282848.explanation: The challenges for Deurne, such as care and housing, are significant. DOE! wants faster permits and better service for residents. Skilled people are needed for this. This means it is not wise to lay off civil servants now.

282849.explanation: DOE! supports residents participating in plans within their own living environment; they know best what is happening and needed. If this strengthens support, longer procedures and higher costs are acceptable.

282850.explanation: DOE! thinks it is a good idea if residents can speak on all topics. At the same time, this requires agreeing on how we seriously consider that input, the rules for the process, and how we as a municipality honestly and timely provide feedback on what happens with the input.

282852.explanation: Deurne is not only a pleasant place to live but also to work. DOE! wants the number of jobs in our municipality to grow along with the number of residents. To achieve this, expanding industrial areas is simply necessary. This prevents Deurne from becoming a 'commuter town.'

282853.explanation: DOE! wants healthy agricultural activity that is good for people, animals, and the environment. But promoting a manure processor does not fit with our vision of a sustainable and safe countryside. When the municipality is asked to cooperate, we want to consider this under strict conditions.

282855.explanation: DOE! values a careful and humane approach over haste; we strive for the best solution for Deurne. 'As soon as possible' policy often leads in practice to large-scale emergency solutions that have little support.

282856.explanation: DOE! consciously does not choose one large reception location. We want small-scale reception places spread across our various villages and neighborhoods. By distributing the reception, each village or neighborhood contributes. This prevents excessive pressure on one specific area.

282857.explanation: The CCD should be a place where residents and associations feel at home and enjoy art and culture together, whether as visitors or creators. Culture is essential for a vibrant Deurne. Therefore, we are willing to invest in a stable foundation for the long term if needed.

282858.explanation: DOE! believes De Wieger is an inseparable part of Deurne. It is one of the most important places that tells our history and gives our municipality its own character. We therefore want to invest specifically in preserving this building so that future generations can also enjoy this heritage.

282860.explanation: DOE! stands for strong and accessible education in our own municipality. We consider it essential that young people in Deurne retain a broad and varied range in secondary education. A gymnasium education is part of that, but it does not necessarily have to be in a new building at the current location now.

282861.explanation: The center is currently not always the attractive place it could be. DOE! wants to give the center a quality boost, and a safer and cleaner area is an essential part of that. We are therefore willing to allow permanent camera surveillance if it improves safety.

282862.explanation: Strong neighborhoods arise where people know each other, help each other, and feel jointly responsible for their living environment. DOE! believes social cohesion and social control are the strongest weapons against insecurity. Neighborhood prevention teams and neighborhood app groups are good examples.

282863.explanation: If a ban is imposed, residents must be able to rely on the government actually enforcing it. However, DOE! does not want to impose only restrictions but offer positive alternatives that bring people together. Therefore, we advocate for a central show organized by the municipality.

282864.explanation: DOE! does not want to impose restrictions only but to offer positive alternatives that bring people together. Therefore, we advocate for a central event organized by the municipality.

282865.explanation: DOE! wants to focus on the bicycle as a good alternative means of transport with the action plan FIETS. For workers, students, and recreational users it is important to be able to ride safely on bike paths. This means that segregated bike paths must be smartly illuminated in the evening.

282866.explanation: DOE! chooses a municipality that grows step by step. After all, no one can see the future; therefore, we want to be able to adjust plans. A ‘scale jump’ should never come at the expense of our unique identity and Deurne qualities. It is crucial that facilities grow along with housing development.

282868.explanation: In the center there are currently buildings already 1 to 6 stories high. We are not opposed in principle to more of such buildings because the housing shortage is high, but the location and integration are decisive.

282869.explanation: For DOE!, quality always comes before quantity. Although we need accelerated housing, we refuse to sacrifice the green and characteristic places in our neighborhoods. Residents have the right to an attractive living environment. We do see room by better utilizing existing buildings.

282870.explanation: DOE! believes the focus must now be on affordability and appropriate supply. By prioritizing first-time buyers and seniors, we stimulate turnover. When seniors move to appropriate housing, family homes become available for others. One new home helps multiple households.

282871.explanation: DOE! wants to build especially in the church villages to keep schools and clubs vital. However, the majority of new homes should be close to existing facilities, such as the station and shops. DOE! wants stepwise growth according to local needs, maintaining the unique character of each center.

282872.explanation: DOE! sees starter loans as a temporary support but is aware of the risks of price inflation. Therefore, this must never be the only solution. Our priority lies in increasing supply. Only by addressing market tightness will homes truly remain affordable.

282873.explanation: DOE! opts for big steps to tackle the housing crisis. Every small initiative helps, but now we must help as many Deurne residents as possible quickly find suitable housing. We are clear about this temporary focus. When space arises, we also embrace smaller-scale initiatives.

282874.explanation: DOE! wants to truly break through the housing crisis. For this, the municipality must regain control. By securing land positions ourselves, we determine what gets built: affordable homes for first-time buyers and seniors. We do this cautiously and stepwise by carefully weighing risks.

282875.explanation: Solar parks and windmills consume a lot of space, which we lack. There are also negative effects on the landscape and living environment. DOE! wants alternatives that yield more returns and have broader support. We also find it important that residents share in the benefits.

282876.explanation: DOE! can imagine the greenhouse horticulture area being used for energy generation, as agreed regionally. However, in our view, multiple uses of the greenhouse area are conceivable; these do not necessarily have to be solar panels.

282877.explanation: DOE! finds companies in the food, energy, and technology sectors conceivable in the greenhouse area. In any case, we think it is important that the use of the greenhouse area contributes to Deurne’s ambitions.

282878.explanation: We do not place windmills just anywhere, but only at designated clustered locations, with the A67 highway being a good site.

282879.explanation: 2050 is not a goal in itself. DOE! does choose a future with sufficient AND clean energy. Instead of focusing only on sun and wind, we prefer to work on new innovative technologies. We also prioritize builders who create homes that generate their own energy.

282880.explanation: DOE! believes that to truly appreciate nature, you must be able to experience it. The Peel is a unique feature of Deurne we are proud of. After long efforts for nature restoration, it is now time to restore balance by investing in experiencing nature.

282881.explanation: DOE! believes that to truly appreciate nature, you must be able to experience it. The Peel is a unique feature of Deurne we are proud of. After long efforts for nature restoration, it is now time to restore balance by investing in experiencing nature.

282883.explanation: DOE! chooses responsible recreation and tourism. Helenaveen and De Peel are gems in our municipality; we want people to enjoy them, but we guard against pressure on livability here.

282884.explanation: Many residents in our villages still truly depend on cars for commuting or care. Therefore, DOE! does not give absolute priority but opts for a healthy balance where we invest heavily in alternatives to allow leaving the car more often, such as with the FIETS action plan.

282886.explanation: For DOE!, traffic safety and good accessibility are necessary. Therefore, we must accelerate the Binderendreef tunnel and add a second safe pedestrian and bicycle tunnel at the station. With municipal growth, an extra tunnel may be necessary to relieve the center.

282887.explanation: DOE! does not want to change the rules for assistance with poverty. Those eligible now must also be able to receive that help in the future.

282888.explanation: DOE! finds it important that youth have a nice and safe place to gather and relax. We initiate the Youth Council Deurne to hear their voice about a youth center. The initial focus: a nice, safe place to unwind for young people.

282889.explanation: DOE! chooses fair distribution of funds available for welfare benefits. We consider it solidarity if you contribute when you earn more than others on welfare. This prevents an uneven playing field.

282893.explanation: DOE! believes that the number of jobs must grow along with our residents. Therefore, we support local entrepreneurs and provide space to expand business parks. Entrepreneurs from outside Deurne must support the local economy when they want to establish themselves here.

282894.explanation: We want Deurne to remain affordable for its residents. Therefore, the property tax (ozb) must be lowered when possible.

282896.explanation: DOE! believes everyone in Deurne counts and should be able to participate. We make no distinction between newcomers and current residents. We consider existing arrangements for this sufficient.

282897.explanation: DOE! believes a strong community arises when everyone gets the chance to participate according to their ability. Participation is essential for us: by doing socially meaningful work, we strengthen social cohesion and the self-reliance of our residents.

282898.explanation: DOE! considers an environmental zone too strict a measure. In some municipal areas, truck bans already exist. We think these should be better enforced.

282901.explanation: DOE! believes everyone should be able to participate. Although we fully understand the necessity of accessibility to these basic products, we believe no separate new policy is needed. After all, there are already distribution points and other poverty arrangements.

282848.explanation: DeurneNU! does not opt for blunt cuts by firing civil servants. A strong municipality means good, accessible services. We focus on efficient work, clear choices, and execution.

282849.explanation: Residents must be involved early and seriously in decisions. At the same time, good governance also requires clarity: no unnecessarily long procedures, delays, or extra costs.

282850.explanation: Residents in Deurne have the right to speak at every meeting, including on non-agenda items. We find this important. At the same time, clear rules ensure an orderly and careful decision-making process.

282852.explanation: Employment must grow with housing. Therefore, a new business park, for example near the A67, is needed, and existing sites must be able to expand. We consciously choose suitable businesses that strengthen Deurne economically and keep it livable.

282853.explanation: We have long advocated a broader view on energy generation. A manure processor fits this: it combines innovation, reuse of residual flows, and local energy production. If well integrated, this can be a valuable addition for Deurne and the region.

282855.explanation: Deurne takes responsibility for asylum reception, but speed must never be the starting point. We choose the Deurne model: small-scale reception, evenly spread over villages and neighborhoods, with attention to livability, support, and local control.

282856.explanation: We stand for the Deurne model: asylum reception small-scale and spread over villages and neighborhoods. Reception locations of limited size create more support, better guidance, and maintain livability. For this, the municipality must also be willing to allocate resources.

282857.explanation: Culture is important, but a cultural center does not have to remain open at all costs. We make choices based on use, quality, and affordability. Public money must be used effectively and requires healthy and realistic operation.

282858.explanation: Museum De Wieger is important for Deurne, but more money is not a given. We make choices based on quality, reach, and effect. Public funds must be deployed efficiently and require healthy operations, not automatic additional funding.

282860.explanation: The gymnasium is an important addition to secondary education offerings in Deurne and must be preserved. We therefore want to think along about future-proof solutions, such as new construction, focusing on quality and efficient use of resources.

282861.explanation: Camera surveillance does not solve problems; at best, it de-escalates and shifts nuisance. DeurneNU! chooses targeted and temporary camera surveillance where safety is under pressure. Permanent surveillance around entertainment areas can be appropriate.

282862.explanation: Neighborhood prevention through neighborhood watches and app groups is not a municipal task. Safety should be organized professionally and centrally. We opt for more police presence on the streets and visible enforcement.

282863.explanation: Rules that apply must be enforced, including the fireworks ban. However, extra strict or intensified enforcement is not an end in itself. DeurneNU! opts for realistic enforcement, focusing on safety and nuisance.

282864.explanation: A municipal fireworks show is a nice idea, especially with a nationwide fireworks ban. It brings people together and ensures a festive New Year's Eve, with attention to costs, safety, and an appropriate arrangement for Deurne and the villages.

282865.explanation: Lighting is important for safety, but not on all cycle paths in the rural area. We choose tailored solutions: good lighting on busy routes, school cycling routes, and places where it is really needed. This way we improve safety without unnecessary costs.

282866.explanation: Housing construction is necessary to keep Deurne livable, but the exact number is not an end in itself. Growth must fit the villages and neighborhoods, with attention to facilities, space, and feasibility. We choose to build where needed, at the right pace but with ambition.

282868.explanation: High-rise buildings can be an efficient solution for the housing shortage in Deurne-Center. However, we set clear frameworks: high-rise only where it fits the surroundings and contributes to livability and more greenery in the neighborhood.

282869.explanation: There are still opportunities for housing construction within the built-up area. At the same time, we find greenery and quality of life important. Therefore, we prefer building on the edges of villages and neighborhoods, with infill where it fits and adds quality.

282870.explanation: Starters, the elderly, and social housing are important, but also homes for families and movers. A healthy mix of housing types is needed to keep neighborhoods livable and allow people in Deurne to progress. Deurne needs homes for all target groups.

282871.explanation: We want substantial construction in the church villages so that young and old can continue to live there. At the same time, housing construction is also necessary in Deurne center and other neighborhoods. It is about a good balance.

282872.explanation: A starter's loan can help young people and starters buy their first home and increases the chance that they remain living in Deurne. It is an instrument used in other municipalities and contributes to an accessible housing market; in Deurne, we should not lag behind.

282873.explanation: Small housing projects are actually important for pace and customization, especially in villages and neighborhoods. DeurneNU! wants to allow space for both large and small projects. The decisive factor should not be size but the contribution to livability and housing needs.

282874.explanation: To speed up housing construction, the municipality needs to take more control. By strategically purchasing land itself, Deurne can steer pace, quality, and affordability. A more active land policy gives the municipality more opportunities to truly get housing construction off the ground.

282875.explanation: DeurneNU! believes we need a broader perspective on energy generation. Besides solar and wind, we want to stimulate innovative technologies such as hydrogen, aquathermy and green gas, so we choose solutions that fit the landscape, support and future-proofing.

282876.explanation: The greenhouse horticulture area along the N270 offers opportunities for energy generation, but not for solar parks. We want to use this location for a mix of innovative energy solutions that fit business activity, infrastructure and the environment.

282877.explanation: The greenhouse horticulture area along the N270 is particularly suitable for modern companies in food, energy and technology. By developing this site smartly, we strengthen innovation, employment and the economic future of Deurne, fitting its location and infrastructure.

282878.explanation: We look at each location to determine if wind energy suits the environment. Not only along highways, but where spatially responsible and neighbors can benefit. Support, safety and landscape integration are decisive.

282879.explanation: We want to take steps towards sustainability, but 'do everything' goes too far. Energy generation must be feasible and fit Deurne. We choose realistic goals, innovation and regional cooperation, without putting landscape and livability under pressure.

282880.explanation: In and around the Deurnese Peel and Mariapeel, enough has already changed in recent years. We find it important that nature remains accessible to residents and tourists. Further intervention is not self-evident and requires critical consideration.

282881.explanation: The Deurnese Peel and Mariapeel are valuable natural areas but must also remain accessible to residents and visitors. Consistently insisting on closure is not the right path. We choose a balance between protection, use and support, rather than further closure.

282883.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs in Helenaveen deserve the same opportunities as the rest of Deurne. Tourism should suit the area but should not be limited in advance to 'only small-scale'. We opt for tailor-made solutions, with space for initiative and development where possible.

282884.explanation: Encouraging walking, cycling and public transport is good, but in a rural municipality like Deurne, the car is often necessary. We opt for accessibility for everyone: safe cycling routes and good public transport where possible, without structurally disadvantaging the car.

282886.explanation: The situation on Liesselseweg is a problem, but an additional road tunnel for cars is not automatically the solution. We want to keep unnecessary traffic out of the center and focus on a broader accessibility approach, including a ring road around Deurne and smart routes for better traffic flow.

282887.explanation: Help must reach people who really need it. Structurally raising income limits is not a solution in itself. We choose tailored and targeted support, so public funds are used effectively and people remain independent where possible.

282888.explanation: A youth center can contribute to good facilities for youth, especially for secondary school-aged young people. This does not necessarily mean new construction: reusing an existing building is also a smart and affordable option.

282889.explanation: Work must pay off. By giving people on social assistance more room to earn extra income, we encourage them to gain work experience and take the step to regular employment. This increases independence and reduces the distance to the labor market.

282893.explanation: Local entrepreneurs, both small and large, are important for Deurne's economy. By giving them priority on industrial estates, we retain employment in Deurne and ensure that growth benefits the local community.

282894.explanation: The burdens on residents and entrepreneurs are already high enough. As a municipality, we must keep a close eye on our expenditures and lower property taxes where possible. Lower burdens keep living and doing business in Deurne affordable.

282896.explanation: Integration is important, but spending more money is not an end in itself. It's about what works: learning the language, participation and employment. We opt for effective integration with clear expectations, so newcomers can participate independently as soon as possible.

282897.explanation: Those who can, may give something back to society. Socially meaningful work helps people build rhythm, experience and self-worth. Of course, we take personal circumstances and health into account; customization remains the basic principle.

282898.explanation: An environmental zone is not suitable for Deurne now and would make the center less accessible. We prefer to promote clean mobility without additional bans. Only when accessibility and connectivity are demonstrably well arranged can this be reconsidered in the future.

282901.explanation: No one should be excluded from basic products due to lack of money. Menstrual products must be accessible to people who cannot afford them. This can be arranged in a targeted and discreet way, so help reaches those who need it.

282848.explanation: Deurne is on the verge of a scale jump. To manage this growth properly and especially to build our community, more rather than fewer civil servants will be needed.

282849.explanation: The involvement of our residents in municipal tasks creates more support and recognizability of the decisions made. Our principle is that this must be easily accessible for every resident.

282850.explanation: Everyone must be able to reach the municipal council quickly and easily. Besides speaking at the council, there are other and sometimes even better options. Such as a weekly consultation hour or walk-in evenings. With more time to engage in good conversations together.

282852.explanation: With the growing population, it is important to maintain the balance between living and working. So that Deurne does not become a municipality that is deserted during the day. For this, it is important to also invest in industrial estates. Larger estates are developed regionally.

282853.explanation: The need for manure processing arises from an excess of animal manure that cannot be used on the land. We therefore consider reducing the number of animals a better solution. This also results in a healthier rural area for people and nature.

282855.explanation: The municipality of Deurne must also comply with the obligations of the Dispersion Act. We have both a legal and a moral obligation to provide shelter for people who need it. It also shows solidarity not to shift the shelter task to other municipalities.

282856.explanation: We must comply with the obligations of the Dispersion Act. When small-scale is not possible, larger-scale shelter is also an option for us. Guidance, care and education for the asylum seekers to be housed are also easier to organize.

282857.explanation: The Culture Center is an important facility for a vibrant and lively municipality. Both for lovers of music, theater and dance and for the rich community life. It provides a place for meeting, inspiration and wonder.

282858.explanation: Museum de Wieger is important cultural and historical heritage of our municipality. It is a unique museum with national appeal where art, history and our local identity come together. Moreover, it is a tourist attraction, which is good for our local economy.

282860.explanation: The Willibrord Gymnasium has a supraregional function. This fits a municipality like Deurne that is growing within the smartest region of the country: Brainport.

282861.explanation: Our safety benefits from a combination of surveillance and prevention. Investing in prevention, for example in youth work, is more important to us. Cameras only record what happens, but do not prevent it. By focusing well on prevention, camera surveillance becomes or will become unnecessary.

282862.explanation: In neighborhood watches and neighborhood app groups, it is important that these are focused on detecting and reporting. Intervention in criminal offenses is reserved for the police and official enforcement officers.

282863.explanation: The nationwide firework ban that has been introduced prevents environmental damage, has broad support among vulnerable groups, and reduces pressure on healthcare. As with any ban, proper enforcement is important.

282864.explanation: Now that a nationwide firework ban has been implemented, it is good for the municipality to organize an alternative. This can foster connection and provide a smooth transition to a New Year's Eve without private fireworks.

282865.explanation: Not all bike paths in rural areas need lighting. Only those places where the safety of cyclists is at risk, especially schoolchildren cycling to school.

282866.explanation: Deurne is in a region that will grow significantly in the coming years. This is an opportunity to maintain important social facilities and keep a thriving community life in our church villages and neighborhoods. It is important, however, that we preserve our village-like and green character.

282868.explanation: The maximum building height is always related to the surroundings and is part of an overall plan for an area. Therefore, the maximum building height in parts of the center can also be lower than six residential floors.

282869.explanation: There is still space for housing construction within the built-up area, though this space is now limited. Therefore, building will also need to take place on the edges of the built-up area and in the countryside, but with preservation of the village-like and green character and strengthening of our beautiful landscapes.

282870.explanation: Houses have become unaffordable for many residents. That is why we prioritize the types of housing with the greatest need: social rental, starter homes, and senior homes. But every housing development also includes room for family homes. A healthy mix leads to a healthy housing market.

282871.explanation: New homes must of course also be built in the church villages. But it makes sense to build the largest number of new homes close to existing amenities, which are concentrated around the center of Deurne and surrounding neighborhoods.

282872.explanation: Housing affordability is under severe pressure. Starter loans can help make a home affordable and may encourage young people to continue living in their village or neighborhood instead of moving outside the municipality.

282873.explanation: Given the enormous need for affordable housing, larger projects can also lead to a greater housing supply. Larger projects are usually financially more robust, so they can contribute to the quality of the living environment.

282874.explanation: We support an active municipality that keeps control over spatial developments such as housing construction. Through active land policy, we determine how our municipality will look, rather than being at the mercy of project developers' wishes.

282875.explanation: There should be no prioritization regarding the generation of sustainable energy. We must do everything and utilize all possibilities to achieve our climate goals.

282876.explanation: For us, the former greenhouse horticulture area is a suitable location for large-scale sustainable energy generation. For solar parks, the principle is that as many panels as possible should first be installed on the roofs of houses, businesses, and social institutions.

282877.explanation: For us, the former greenhouse horticulture area is a logical place for generating sustainable energy. If this is not (timely) possible, a new work location is conceivable, with circularity and sustainability being paramount.

282878.explanation: Given the need to take rapid steps to meet climate goals, locations for wind turbines cannot be excluded in advance.

282879.explanation: It is necessary for the climate to save energy and generate it sustainably as soon as possible, and to invest in storage of generated energy. We would even favor accelerating the goal to be energy neutral to 2040.

282880.explanation: The Deurnese Peel and Mariapeel form a unique but vulnerable natural area that we as a municipality must cherish. Nature cannot protect itself and needs a helping hand.

282881.explanation: The Peel already has zoning to restrict vulnerable nature areas from visitors/recreationists. We support this. It is important to maintain a balance between nature conservation and the support for it. Experiencing nature is also necessary for that.

282883.explanation: When utilizing the tourism potential of Helenaveen and the Peel, the carrying capacity of these areas must be carefully considered. Large-scale tourism does not fit there.

282884.explanation: Priority for other forms of transport than the car is not only good for the environment and air quality but also promotes health. Of course, giving priority to walking, cycling, and public transport does not mean the car is no longer important in Deurne.

282886.explanation: A second rail tunnel may eventually be desirable, but for us, the timely and correct realization of the first rail tunnel now takes priority. The locations for a potential second rail tunnel is a question for later.

282887.explanation: Income limits no longer correspond to the current economic reality. As a result, more people are just above the official poverty line but still have great difficulty making ends meet with their monthly income. Security of existence for all residents is very important to us.

282888.explanation: Young people need their own safe and accessible place that supports their development, creatively, musically, and socially. A youth center gives youth workers the opportunity to offer preventive and tailored support.

282889.explanation: Increasing the income space alongside social assistance benefits results in more human dignity, incentives toward paid work, less poverty, more income stability, and higher chances of leaving social assistance.

282893.explanation: The type and the added economic and social value of a company determine establishment on business parks, not the origin. Innovative companies and startups of new economic drivers deserve priority.

282894.explanation: Reducing property tax (OZB) is not a goal in itself. Our municipality has big ambitions that must be financed. The level of the property tax is a means to provide for this. It is important to us to remain aligned with neighboring municipalities.

282896.explanation: Everyone counts for us, including newcomers. To ensure they can quickly participate fully in our society, integration is highly important. We must invest in this and make use of the good volunteer initiatives already present.

282897.explanation: Supporting residents with social assistance benefits is our priority. There must be room for customization so that everyone contributes according to their ability. We must also consider those who, due to personal circumstances, are unable to provide a counter-service.

282898.explanation: Good air quality is important for the health of people and nature. Deurne still faces a major challenge here. A low emission zone can help us comply quickly with standards for clean and healthy air.

282901.explanation: Access to basic daily products is not self-evident for everyone. We support our residents who need this with free distribution of these care products.

282848.explanation: CDA Deurne chooses for better service delivery, less external hiring, and more efficient working; dismissal is not a goal in itself.

282849.explanation: Strong position for village and neighborhood councils with input via residents and attention to accessibility of civil servants, but also a focus on less bureaucracy and swift implementation.

282850.explanation: At present, people can speak on agenda items. This can improve. For non-agenda topics, we propose having one agenda item per quarter at council meetings for free speaking on diverse subjects.

282852.explanation: Targeted expansion adjacent to Kranenmortel and reorganization. This means relocating nuisance-causing high-category companies to a new recycling and energy center. No new XL distribution.

282853.explanation: Relocation of heavy category companies (including manure processing) to a new recycling and energy center near N270; positive about green gas/manure digestion.

282855.explanation: In our program, we indicate keeping the 1% norm per neighborhood/village as a starting point. This probably provides sufficient space for the national task the municipality of Deurne has. In addition, working with flexible housing for this issue instead of social rental housing.

282856.explanation: Here too, the 1% norm applies as a starting point with the aim of realizing small-scale shelter. This better supports integration.

282857.explanation: Revision of the subsidy system and professionalization of the management of social real estate via Deurnese Management Foundation; not automatically 'more subsidy'.

282858.explanation: Professionalization of real estate management through a Management Foundation and support; no explicit commitment to 'more money'.

282860.explanation: Investing in facilities and (primary) schools per community is important to us. We look at more cooperation between the Gymnasium on one hand and Peelland College on the other to offer the right education in the right place (large-scale versus small-scale).

282861.explanation: Emphasis on neighborhood prevention and traffic/social safety. Cameras are a possibility, but at the same time we must also ensure there is sufficient supervision by police and enforcement officers.

282862.explanation: Certainly: expansion of neighborhood prevention with neighborhood watch groups and community app groups.

282863.explanation: Lighting fireworks is a longstanding tradition for people. Room for initiatives to organize fireworks shows to locally celebrate New Year's Eve together in this way.

282864.explanation: We see room for initiatives in the various local communities to give form to this.

282865.explanation: Safe cycling routes and improved lighting where necessary; no generic 'lighting of all paths'.

282866.explanation: 8,500 homes is a regional task; Deurne makes a significant contribution but the figure is not allocated one-to-one to Deurne. By making a strong contribution we ensure that facilities in our municipality remain sustainable in the long term. This can help solve the nuisance on Milhezerweg.

282868.explanation: CDA chooses an appropriate scale and integrated redevelopment.

282869.explanation: Combination of limited inner-city projects and large expansion locations. In particular, the corner of Molenstraat/Kerkstraat offers possibilities for building homes with sufficient attention to greenery.

282870.explanation: Standards for new expansions: 30% starter homes, 20% seniors, 30% affordable, 20% free sector. We must build according to need, also looking at the long term.

282871.explanation: Both new neighborhoods near the Deurne core and building in church villages; balance is leading.

282872.explanation: It is important that construction is affordable. That is why we also choose to realize 30% starter homes and 30% affordable houses in plans.

282873.explanation: A mix of large expansions and small initiatives is very important (CPO, tiny houses, housing splits). Small projects can be realized relatively quickly and contribute in the short term. Larger projects in the longer term.

282874.explanation: Active land policy with land positions and pre-emption rights; within a realistic budget policy.

282875.explanation: Positive about, among other things, manure digestion and sustainability; we do not explicitly choose against solar/wind but for 'doing what works'.

282876.explanation: We see this area as best suited for the realization of a recycling and energy hub (possibly combined with greenhouse horticulture).

282877.explanation: We see this area as best suited for the realization of a recycling and energy hub. This can strengthen economic clusters/employment.

282878.explanation: No explicit wind location position in our program. We see little opportunities for Deurne. Should there be possibilities along the A67, we can agree with that.

282879.explanation: Realistic, rewarding climate policy and support for sustainability.

282880.explanation: Commitment to greenery/water, integrated fauna management and recreational accessibility; no specific extra Peel restoration agenda but focus on extensification of agriculture around the Peel.

282881.explanation: Seek a balance between nature, recreation, and livability.

282883.explanation: Space for small-scale (stay) recreation fitting nature and village character.

282884.explanation: Strong focus on cyclists/pedestrians, but also free parking and investments in roads; balance instead of absolute priority. In the center, consider 'car as guest' constructions.

282886.explanation: A tunnel on Liesselseweg will inevitably lead to large traffic flow through the center. This will cause severe nuisance in the center from through traffic that does not need to be there.

282887.explanation: Our program contains measures for minimum-income families (sports/culture). The current threshold used is appropriate for us.

282888.explanation: The CDA wants more meeting places for young people, but no specific plan for a new youth center.

282889.explanation: Language support and emphasis on social contribution, without harsh penalties, unless there is unwillingness.

282893.explanation: Support for existing entrepreneurs and clustering to create better cohesion and structure on business parks.

282894.explanation: Our line is cautious with no automatic increases except for inflation; no active reductions are planned.

282896.explanation: Language support and spreading of status holders for better integration.

282897.explanation: We emphasize willingness to make a social contribution.

282898.explanation: This seems unfeasible to us for a rural municipality.

282901.explanation: There are subsidized initiatives that provide for this, such as Pompidom and the Food Bank.

282848.explanation: The municipality must focus on what it exists for, such as safety, housing and accessibility. Fewer civil servants and fewer rules means lower costs, faster decisions and more space for residents and entrepreneurs.

282849.explanation: Residents must be heard, but decision-making must not get stuck. Extra procedures make policy more expensive and slower. Deurne needs speed in housing and economy.

282850.explanation: Participation must be serious and structured. Free speaking on everything undermines effective governance. The council must be able to make decisions instead of endless meetings without results.

282852.explanation: Without space for businesses there are no jobs and no facilities. New business parks create employment, retain young people in Deurne and strengthen the local economy.

282853.explanation: A modern manure processor is not a problem but a solution. Less transport, more sustainability and support for our agricultural sector. That is realistic environmental policy.

282855.explanation: Deurne must take its legal responsibility, but not by acting hastily. Carefulness, support and livability are leading. By first carefully weighing locations and making agreements, we maintain control and prevent emergency shelters that put pressure on society.

282856.explanation: Small-scale shelter is more manageable and increases support among local residents. This keeps care humane and clear, without overburdening neighborhoods.

282857.explanation: Culture is important, but structural subsidies without reform are not a solution. Institutions must have their operations in order, generate their own income, and work more efficiently.

282858.explanation: Deurne puts itself on the map with good accessibility, housing construction, and business activity. Extra subsidies to a single institution for a very selective target group yield too little return for the entire municipality.

282860.explanation: An agreement has been made with the gymnasium stating that it is responsible for new construction (with an annual financial contribution from the municipality). Agreements are agreements. Just like residents and entrepreneurs, responsibility must be taken for what has been agreed.

282861.explanation: Camera surveillance is a severe measure that should only be used temporarily and targeted. Structural safety is achieved through enforcement and prevention, not permanent Big Brother monitoring in the center.

282862.explanation: Safety starts in the neighborhood. By supporting neighborhood prevention, we strengthen social control and prevent crime without expensive additional enforcement.

282863.explanation: If there are rules, they must be enforced. Otherwise, they might as well be abolished. Strict enforcement prevents injury, nuisance, and damage to emergency services and property.

282864.explanation: A fireworks show is fun but not a core task. The municipality is not an event agency. That money is better spent on safety and facilities. However, local initiatives can be supported, for example with permits or financial contributions.

282865.explanation: Lighting where necessary is sensible, but lighting everywhere is expensive and harmful to nature and landscape. Tailored solutions are better than a general mandate.

282866.explanation: Growth is not a threat, but a condition to maintain facilities and to add new ones. Without housing construction, Deurne ages and loses its attractive character.

282868.explanation: Rigid rules are restrictive. Height should fit the location and surroundings, not a fixed political maximum.

282869.explanation: Preserving livability requires building on the edges, not filling every green spot. That means making choices.

282870.explanation: Housing turnover is essential. Without affordable homes for starters and seniors, the housing market remains stuck.

282871.explanation: The core of Deurne is best equipped for a substantial increase in residents. However, housing in the church villages keeps them livable, so a substantial number must also be built there. This ensures schools, clubs, and shops continue to exist.

282872.explanation: Starters loans sound appealing but drive up prices and mainly benefit sellers. The solution lies in building more, not extra subsidies.

282873.explanation: Large projects deliver many homes faster. This is necessary to truly tackle the housing shortage.

282874.explanation: The municipality is not a project developer. Risks belong to market parties; the government sets frameworks and speeds up procedures.

282875.explanation: First focus on solar panels on roofs and innovation. This prevents cluttering of the landscape and increases support.

282876.explanation: This area is intended for economic development and jobs, not for large-scale solar fields.

282877.explanation: This strengthens employment, innovation, and the economic future of Deurne.

282878.explanation: This limits nuisance for residents and damage to landscape and nature.

282879.explanation: Ambition is good, but affordability and feasibility must come first. Goals should not burden residents with high costs.

282880.explanation: Nature restoration is primarily the task of the province and land managers. The municipality must prevent entrepreneurs and residents from facing extra restrictions.

282881.explanation: Protection is important, but closing off harms recreation and support. A balance between nature and accessibility is better than closure.

282883.explanation: Small-scale tourism suits the character of the area and prevents mass disturbance.

282884.explanation: In a rural municipality, the car remains essential for work and family. Ideology should not stand in the way of accessibility.

282886.explanation: The more grade-separated crossings at the railway, the better. They improve accessibility and traffic safety. However, if we must choose where, we would rather advocate for an eastern ring road around Deurne with a railway tunnel.

282887.explanation: Aid must remain targeted at those who really need it. Increasing it generically makes the system unaffordable.

282888.explanation: Young people need their own space, as the municipal council also decided in the youth work vision. This prevents nuisance and strengthens social bonds.

282889.explanation: Work must pay off. This increases independence and reduces dependence on the government.

282893.explanation: A level playing field is fairer and better for economic growth.

282894.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs should be able to spend their money themselves instead of handing it over to the municipality.

282896.explanation: Integration starts with personal effort. Extra subsidies do not solve that.

282897.explanation: Participation is better than standing on the sidelines. It increases self-worth and involvement.

282898.explanation: This harms accessibility and businesses without demonstrable local effect.

282901.explanation: Aid should flow through existing poverty schemes, not via separate free products.

282848.explanation: The municipal organization has grown significantly in recent years. We want a lean, efficient municipality, but not blind layoffs. First, critically examine tasks, hiring, and processes to maintain good service in Deurne.

282849.explanation: Not just hearing, but really listening. Early in the process, with clear rules and one clear decision moment. This prevents hassle and ultimately saves time and money.

282850.explanation: Public speaking is part of an open council. Make it accessible, with clear speaking times and order so that everyone can be heard, even if something is not yet on the agenda.

282852.explanation: Business parks are necessary but not scattered everywhere. Growth is possible. But it's better to cluster and provide good access in a few logical locations than to keep adding new ‘mosaic pieces’ to the landscape.

282853.explanation: The municipality can make this possible; whether it should be specifically encouraged is less important.

282855.explanation: In the interest of both local residents and the asylum seekers themselves, it is better to do this carefully. Transparant Deurne is therefore the foundation of the 'Deurne Model'. It can be done when it fits within the agreed small scale. This is more important than the number of beds.

282856.explanation: Transparant Deurne is the foundation of the 'Deurne Model'. We are convinced that small scale is better for both local residents and asylum seekers. We also shelter Ukrainians on a small scale. The costs do not have to be higher and should always be borne by the national government.

282857.explanation: Every day the CCD starts with 1000 euros in subsidy. By the end of the day, the money is gone again. Money is not the solution. The municipality must mainly ensure that the CCD remains open by ensuring good management.

282858.explanation: Sufficient funds have been allocated for Museum De Wieger. More money is not proportional to necessary investments in, for example, sports clubs and other social facilities.

282860.explanation: Retaining the gymnasium is good for Deurne and the region. The municipality is responsible for school accommodation: we must cooperate with new construction/modernization if necessary.

282861.explanation: CCTV surveillance can help in the center. It is a heavy measure. Outside the center, we are not in favor of extensive CCTV surveillance. The rule there is: temporarily where necessary, with proven effect and clear evaluation; safety mainly requires visible enforcement (police) and targeted measures.

282862.explanation: Neighborhood prevention is a valuable addition to police and enforcement. It must be possible in every neighborhood. The starting point is to support this, for example by ensuring short lines between volunteers and the police. The mayor must regulate this well.

282863.explanation: In case of nuisance, enforcement can be applied, but the prohibition itself does not need to be strictly enforced. In a village like Deurne, this is not a problem as it is in big cities.

282864.explanation: A municipality must stick to its core tasks. If entrepreneurs or a village organize this themselves, the municipality must facilitate and subsidize it through event subsidies. Then the organization happens much more efficiently.

282865.explanation: Continuous cycling routes in the rural area must be better illuminated. But Deurne also has a large network of recreational routes. Around the Peel and through the forests. In these areas, darkness should be cherished. Therefore, 'all' is too broad.

282866.explanation: This is unrealistic, especially until 2040. We certainly need to build, but this goes hand in hand with facilities and good infrastructure. Heiakker counts 2000 homes, Koolhof 1800. And these 8500 are thus more than twice as many.

282868.explanation: A maximum of 6 residential floors fits the scale of the center and prevents 'urban' high-rise buildings. It provides space for densification, but with limits.

282869.explanation: Many young people are eager for starter homes. Construction projects within the village center often proceed slowly. Outside the built-up area, the housing crisis can be solved much faster.

282870.explanation: The greatest pressure is on starters, social rental, and housing for the elderly. That should be the focus, so that flow starts and housing becomes accessible again. This does not exclude the construction of family and more luxurious homes. In practice, mixed projects often get off the ground faster.

282871.explanation: Not 'especially' in the church villages: building also belongs with facilities and public transport in the center. In the villages, yes, where it logically fits and has support.

282872.explanation: A starter loan sounds sympathetic, but helps limitedly if there are too few affordable homes and can drive up prices. Preferably: build faster, affordable plans, and fewer procedural barriers.

282873.explanation: Equal rights. Small projects and initiatives should get a chance: they are faster, more diverse, and often better fit the environment. Favoring only large projects makes building dependent on a few big players.

282874.explanation: With active land policy, the municipality takes back control: on pace, affordability, and quality. That is allowed, provided there are strict financial frameworks and maximum transparency about risks.

282875.explanation: Focus on innovation and smart solutions (savings, storage, roofs, local grids) instead of reflexive large solar fields and wind turbines. Give geothermal energy or heat storage higher priority. These have less impact on the environment.

282876.explanation: The greenhouse horticulture area along the N270 is too valuable to be filled by default with solar parks. Solar energy belongs first on roofs, parking lots, and residual sites.

282877.explanation: This is an excellent location for business activity with little nuisance for the surroundings and good traffic accessibility. It strengthens employment and Deurne's live-work balance.

282878.explanation: If wind turbines come, preferably clustered along the highway: there the nuisance is relatively smaller. No standalone turbines 'in the landscape', close to villages or close to nature. However, we prefer no wind turbines at all.

282879.explanation: 'Doing everything' and becoming fully energy neutral within the municipal boundaries is too absolute and not realistically controllable. We focus on saving and as much clean generation as possible, but without symbolic goals that are unachievable.

282880.explanation: Hundreds of millions have been invested in recent years in nature restoration and creating desirable nature. Maybe nature can sometimes just be left alone and allowed to run its own course.

282881.explanation: The Peel is hardly accessible anymore. While the church villages have traditionally been strongly connected to the Peel. We must move toward a human-inclusive form of nature protection.

282883.explanation: The Peel area requires protection, but 'only on a small scale' is too limiting and locks it down. We want to steer tourism on quality and carrying capacity: where possible small scale, where better accessible and organized, with clear boundaries for nature and livability.

282884.explanation: Walking/cycling/public transport cannot be given 'priority' everywhere: many residents depend on the car. We choose balance: a safe cycling network and better public transport where possible, without pushing out the car.

282886.explanation: A rail tunnel is desirable at the Liesselseweg as well for safety and accessibility. This must be linked to external funding and realistic planning. Much faster and with better financiers than at the Binderendreef, where we now pay five times as much ourselves.

282887.explanation: Increasing the income limit generically is expensive and not always targeted. Preferably: tailor-made solutions, debt prevention, and quick help for people who truly fall between the cracks.

282888.explanation: After the closure of OJC De Roos, there must be a safe, low-threshold place for young people again. This can also be in cooperation with education, sports, and volunteers, but something must return.

282889.explanation: Earning extra money must pay off. It helps people out of social assistance and prevents the poverty trap. Provide space with clear rules, so that working always results in benefits.

282893.explanation: We participate in a large regional business park. Locally, you can thereby choose local entrepreneurs. That is good for employment and community ties. This can be done by smartly facilitating local entrepreneurship within the rules (plot policy, criteria, phasing), without arbitrariness.

282894.explanation: Property tax must go down: charges must remain affordable. This is only possible if the municipality simultaneously makes choices in spending and works more efficiently.

282896.explanation: More spending does not automatically mean better integration. Focus on what works: language, work, participation. With clear expectations and result-oriented policy.

282897.explanation: A counter-performance can help if it is socially useful and appropriate. Not punishment, but tailor-made: what someone can do, with guidance and exceptions where necessary. Many people experience more meaning with a meaningful role in society.

282898.explanation: We see an environmental zone as a step towards more policy to exclude cars from the center. Prefer smart measures: separate through traffic from destination traffic and guide/redirect freight traffic. This way we reduce nuisance and emissions without restricting residents and entrepreneurs.

282901.explanation: We choose efficiency. Distributing free products by the municipality is yet another scheme with organizational costs. Prefer to help via existing poverty schemes and partners, so no one misses out but it remains targeted and efficient.

282848.explanation: 50Plus, as a socially middle party, is fundamentally against (mass) dismissal of civil servants anyway, but the municipality should not limit itself to its core tasks. That smells of the classic liberalism of 'laissez faire laissez aller'. The municipality is undergoing substantial scaling up and then all hands must be on deck.

282849.explanation: We have a representative democracy and not a constitutional or corrective referendum. Nevertheless, in addition to administrative objections, appeals, and higher appeals, we do support a certain degree of direct democracy and participation.

282850.explanation: 50Plus is always in favor of this to promote the democratic process.

282853.explanation: Provided it does not cause nuisance (Stop the stench).

282855.explanation: But in compliance with the Deurne model (maximum 1% of the local population per neighborhood or village).

282856.explanation: We support this based on the so-called 1% norm, also known as the Deurne model.

282857.explanation: The Culture Center is an indispensable meeting place for various activities and also because of its role as a concert hall and for other events.

282858.explanation: De Wieger, together with the Willibrordus Church as well as the Twaalf Apostelen and the four Evangelists in Zeilberg, is the most impressive cultural heritage.

282860.explanation: The gymnasium MUST be preserved for Deurne.

282861.explanation: As long as there is reasonable cause for it.

282863.explanation: Allowed within associations, provided there is a notification obligation.

282864.explanation: Just to combat illegal fireworks alone, we support a fireworks display.

282866.explanation: Housing construction is very essential, but the fact that we are missing out on 19 million in subsidies is now a financial blow. Therefore, the municipality must moderate its ambition level on this matter.

282868.explanation: It depends on the location. The Elkerliek branch is absolutely not disturbing, so high-rise buildings in such locations are definitely possible.

282869.explanation: Indeed, no excessive densification, but new construction is possible through clearance, such as at the site where the De Wiemel swimming pool is currently located.

282870.explanation: Building according to the needs of the different population groups.

282872.explanation: Provided it is responsible in terms of municipal finances (solvency and liquidity).

282873.explanation: Both should be possible and are not mutually exclusive.

282874.explanation: Proactivity in spatial planning is positive, but caution is indeed the watchword here.

282875.explanation: Solar panels only on roofs, but no solar fields, and no wind turbines near residences.

282876.explanation: Absolutely no solar fields, only solar panels on roofs.

282877.explanation: Provided there is perspective for this.

282878.explanation: Provided they are far away from residences.

282879.explanation: Simply striving for this is excellent policy.

282880.explanation: These are magnificent natural areas and we must cherish them.

282881.explanation: This is necessary for the protection and preservation of those natural areas.

282883.explanation: Allow in a reasonable way. No mass tourism but not necessarily only on a small scale either. Continue to proactively monitor developments in consultation with the local population.

282884.explanation: This must be consistently implemented, so also consistently at all roundabouts concerning cyclists.

282886.explanation: The double intersection with traffic lights and the railway crossing together are by far the biggest traffic barrier in Deurne.

282887.explanation: Provided it is financially reasonably responsible. A social safety net yes, but no social hammock.

282888.explanation: Provided there is indisputable need for it.

282889.explanation: The Participation Act and related general administrative measures apply here.

282893.explanation: In principle yes, but not too absolute. Economic interests including employment should come first.

282894.explanation: At least not increase in real terms anymore. Municipal taxes are quite high in Deurne.

282896.explanation: That really depends on the situation and the target groups, also considering the financial consequences for the municipality of Deurne.

282897.explanation: That is a matter for higher government, including formal and material legislators.

282898.explanation: Provided any necessary exemptions are observed.

282901.explanation: Provided this can be irrefutably demonstrated.

282848.explanation: The municipality must improve its services and show more decisiveness.

282849.explanation: Procedures become shorter and cheaper when residents can participate seriously.

282850.explanation: New politics is done together with all residents of Deurne.

282852.explanation: We want to allow room for housing construction and the improvement of existing business parks.

282853.explanation: If it succeeds to agree with entrepreneurs and residents on a suitable location that does not cause nuisance, we can support this.

282855.explanation: When it comes to speed: disagree, we support careful reception aligned with local possibilities.

282856.explanation: We are advocates of small-scale reception, tailored to local possibilities, matching the maximum number of people to the capacity of an area.

282857.explanation: In our view, the municipality has the responsibility to provide such a meeting facility.

282858.explanation: It is important that other social facilities in Deurne center, neighborhoods, and villages also receive sufficient funding.

282860.explanation: It is important to preserve this school for Deurne.

282861.explanation: The problem shifts to other locations; it must be addressed at the core.

282862.explanation: It is important that the municipality supports and appreciates these neighborhood watches and WhatsApp groups.

282863.explanation: Residents should be able to set off fireworks responsibly; vulnerable groups must be protected against heavy fireworks.

282864.explanation: Waste of money. Residents should be able to set off fireworks responsibly.

282866.explanation: This number of 8,500 homes means an increase of approximately 17,500 residents by 2040. Totally unfeasible. It is important to build gradually, with support and the right facilities.

282868.explanation: We remain a village; we want to preserve the character of a village.

282869.explanation: Better on the edges of Deurne. There must be enough space left within the built-up area for facilities like greenery, playgrounds, and meeting places.

282870.explanation: Plots must also be allocated to enable people to build their own homes.

282871.explanation: Should be spread across Deurne center, neighborhoods, and villages.

282872.explanation: Good to help first-time buyers get started.

282873.explanation: Small projects are important for livable neighborhoods and diverse housing supply.

282874.explanation: This enables us to realize more affordable homes.

282875.explanation: We are not in favor of solar parks and large wind turbines.

282876.explanation: We are not in favor of large solar parks.

282877.explanation: Several developments are possible for a valuable use of this area.

282878.explanation: Not desirable to place large wind turbines close to homes (population centers).

282880.explanation: Important to preserve the existing nature in and around the Peel. This is more a provincial and national responsibility.

282881.explanation: Important that residents have access to the Peel.

282883.explanation: Tourism must align with the carrying capacity of an area.

282884.explanation: In the center of Deurne, and village cores, among others to increase safety.

282886.explanation: To improve the flow of traffic.

282887.explanation: We must better support people towards independent living.

282888.explanation: Maintain a youth facility for young people aged 12 to 16.

282889.explanation: Encourage people to return to work and increase financial independence.

282893.explanation: Local entrepreneurs often have stronger social ties with Deurne.

282896.explanation: Important to allocate sufficient funds for this.

282897.explanation: We support socially meaningful work by people with welfare benefits. However, not for everyone, since some are unable to do so.

282898.explanation: To promote the living and residential environment.

282901.explanation: Every woman must have access to this.

statements.title: All bicycle paths in the rural area should be equipped with lighting.

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.pro: Lighting makes it safer for cyclists. To encourage cycling, bike paths must be made as pleasant as possible.
statements.con: Not all cycle paths need lighting, only busy routes. It is better for nature if some places remain unlit.

statements.title: The municipality should provide more subsidies to ensure that the Deurne Cultural Center remains open.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet er met meer

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: voor zorgen dat het Cultuurcentrum Deurne open blijft.

statements.pro: The center is indispensable for art, culture and events in Deurne. The municipality can ensure a stable financial basis for the center through subsidies.
statements.con: More money is not the solution; the center’s management must be improved. The center should generate more of its own income.

statements.title: Deurne should develop more and larger business parks.
statements.pro: Expanding business parks creates more employment for the residents of Deurne.
statements.con: The space should be used for housing.

statements.title: The municipality should do more to restore the nature around the Deurnese Peel and Mariapeel.
statements.moreInfo: Deurnese Peel and Mariapeel are peat bog areas. The nature here is under pressure, partly due to drying out and nitrogen deposition. Recreation is also a problem for recovering peat bogs, both for vulnerable plants and for animal species such as cranes and badgers. The municipality can promote nature restoration, for example by steering recreation away from vulnerable areas, by closing areas, or by consulting with the Water Board about regulating groundwater levels.
statements.pro: These are vulnerable natural areas that need protection. This nature is a unique feature of Deurne and must be preserved.
statements.con: Nature restoration is a task for the national government, the province and land managers. The emphasis should be on making the area accessible for recreation, for Deurne residents and tourists.

statements.title: The temporary extra camera surveillance in the center of Deurne should become permanent.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: in het centrum van Deurne moet blijvend worden.

statements.pro: Camera surveillance makes the center safer. Camera surveillance makes the center cleaner. In the nightlife area it is good to have permanent cameras that record what happens.
statements.con: It is better to invest in preventing nuisance, so camera surveillance is not needed. Visible enforcement works better, such as more police officers or municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). The nuisance in the center will shift to other places as a result.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.pro: A starter loan can make a home affordable for young people. A starter loan increases the chance that young people can continue to live in Deurne.
statements.con: This possibility will cause house prices to rise. Such a loan has no effect if there are too few houses. Building more is the best solution to make housing affordable.

statements.title: To save costs, the municipality should lay off civil servants and focus primarily on its core tasks.

titleParts.text: Om kosten te besparen moet de gemeente ambtenaren ontslaan en zich vooral richten op haar

titleParts.glossary: The tasks for which the municipality is legally responsible, such as safety and the maintenance of public spaces.

statements.moreInfo: Core tasks of the municipality are social affairs such as the Social Support Act (Wmo), social assistance benefits, the Youth Act and participation; assessing spatial plans and housing; issuing permits; public order and safety; management of public space; and civil affairs such as passports and municipal registration. Tasks that are not core include, for example, creating sports and play facilities (playgrounds, swimming pools, sports clubs), culture (library, museums, cultural center, music associations), community facilities (community centers), and economic policy.
statements.pro: In addition to lower costs, this also means decisions are made more quickly. The municipality should focus on safety, housing and accessibility.
statements.con: Laying off civil servants will harm service and provision. Due to the municipality’s growth, more civil servants are needed, not fewer.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in new construction to keep the gymnasium in Deurne.
statements.pro: It is important to keep a gymnasium (classical secondary school) among the secondary school options in Deurne. The municipality is responsible for school accommodation.
statements.con: The municipality already gives yearly funding to the gymnasium, which could be used for the new building. A new building is not necessarily needed at the moment.

statements.title: The municipality should involve residents more in decision-making, even if that means procedures become longer and more expensive.
statements.pro: Residents themselves know best what is happening in their living environment and what is needed. In that way we increase support for municipal decisions. More involvement and input from residents can ultimately save time and money.
statements.con: Decision-making is already democratic enough, because the municipal council represents residents and there are village and neighborhood councils. Good governance means avoiding unnecessary delays or costs. Some decisions, for example about housing, need to be made quickly.

statements.title: The municipality should accommodate the legally required number of asylum seekers as soon as possible. That number is 191.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk het wettelijk verplichte aantal

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.moreInfo: The number 191 comes from the Distribution Act and is therefore a nationally imposed obligation. In Deurne additional policy was made about the distribution of these places in the municipality: the so-called "1% rule." The policy is that per population center a maximum of 1% of the number of inhabitants may be housed. For example: about 1,900 people live in Neerkant, which means a maximum of 19 asylum seekers can be accommodated.
statements.pro: We have, besides a legal duty, a moral obligation to receive refugees. The municipality should not push the reception of refugees onto other municipalities.
statements.con: When searching for a suitable shelter location, careful consideration and public support are more important than speed. Small-scale shelter fits better in the municipality of Deurne.

statements.title: Deurne must build roughly 8,500 additional homes in total by 2040 (a major expansion).
statements.moreInfo: The "Schaalsprong" refers to the growth of the Eindhoven region. The knowledge-intensive manufacturing industry and the region's popularity as a place to live and work mean the region is growing rapidly. There is therefore a great demand for more housing, with the accompanying growth of business parks, mobility and other facilities.
statements.pro: This number of homes is needed to prevent the municipality of Deurne from aging. This growth is necessary to maintain services in Deurne.
statements.con: The exact number is not an end in itself; plans can be adjusted. Deurne must retain its village-like and green character; 8,500 additional homes would threaten that identity.

statements.title: Museum De Wieger must receive more funding to put Deurne on the map.
statements.pro: The museum is important cultural-historical heritage of the municipality of Deurne and must be preserved. Museum De Wieger attracts tourists, which is good for the local economy.
statements.con: Putting Deurne on the map is better achieved in other ways: for example, investing in good accessibility and business activity. Enough money has already been spent on Museum De Wieger.

statements.title: Residents must be allowed to speak at every council meeting, including on topics that are not on the agenda.
statements.moreInfo: At the moment the right to speak in the municipal council and in council committees is only allowed when it concerns topics that are on the agenda.
statements.pro: This way residents can reach the municipal council quickly. This makes it easy for residents to make their voices heard.
statements.con: The risk is that meetings will become too unstructured. It is sufficient to hold a council meeting with this option once a quarter.

statements.title: The municipality must encourage the construction of a manure processor between Deurne and Venray.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stimuleren dat er een

titleParts.glossary: An installation that produces gas from livestock manure.

statements.moreInfo: A manure processor is an installation in which animal manure is processed into other products, such as biogas. This means the manure is not used to fertilize farmland and the manure surplus is reduced. Farmers from the area bring their manure to the processor. There are different variants of manure processors, for example mono-digestion of cow manure, or co-digestion where a mix of pig manure and green waste is used. The size of an installation can also differ.
statements.pro: A manure processor can generate energy when manure is converted into biogas. A manure processor reduces pollution of soil or water and is therefore better for the environment.
statements.con: A manure processor is a solution for the surplus of manure. Fewer animals on the land is a better solution. A manure processor causes nuisance for residents, such as odor.

statements.title: In Deurne-Centrum, residential complexes may have a maximum of six storeys.
statements.pro: With this maximum number of storeys we preserve the village character of Deurne-Centrum.
statements.con: In some locations, building higher is possible and does not have to be intrusive.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) in Deurne must be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.pro: Lower taxes make Deurne more attractive to live and do business in. The municipality should scrutinize spending and be more frugal.
statements.con: This tax is necessary to maintain services. Deurne should keep its tax level comparable to surrounding municipalities.

statements.title: In Helenaveen and the Peel area the municipality should only promote tourism on a small scale.
statements.pro: Large-scale tourism would disturb and damage the nature. Large-scale tourism does not fit the village character and would harm liveability.
statements.con: This is too restrictive a measure that leaves too little room for initiatives. Businesses in these areas should have the same opportunities as businesses elsewhere in the municipality.

statements.title: There is no more space for housing development within Deurne’s built-up area.

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

titleParts.text: van Deurne is geen ruimte meer voor woningbouw.

statements.pro: We want to preserve the greenery in the neighborhoods (parks, green spaces, playgrounds).
statements.con: In some places, old buildings can make way for housing.

statements.title: The municipality must give priority to walking, cycling and public transport over car use.
statements.pro: Less car use is good for the environment and air quality. More walking and cycling is good for the health of our residents.
statements.con: In a rural municipality like Deurne, a car is indispensable for many residents to get to work. A car is indispensable for many residents when they need to go to the GP or hospital.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to prioritise large housing projects over smaller projects of fewer than six homes.
statements.pro: Large projects deliver many homes at once and therefore make a relatively large contribution to solving the housing shortage.
statements.con: Small projects can be realized faster and therefore relatively quickly contribute to solving the housing shortage.

statements.title: The municipality should only cooperate on wind turbines if they are placed along the highway.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet alleen meewerken aan

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

titleParts.text: als ze langs de snelweg komen te staan.

statements.pro: In that way we limit the nuisance for residents. In that way we prevent damage to the landscape and nature as much as possible.
statements.con: To meet climate targets we will need to install wind turbines in more places. The municipality can also seek other locations where local residents can share in the benefits.

statements.title: The income threshold for poverty assistance (such as exceptional financial aid) must be raised so that more people receive financial support.

titleParts.glossary: A sum of money for people with low incomes to cover extra or high costs, for example for a washing machine.

titleParts.text: ) moeten omhoog, zodat meer mensen financiële hulp krijgen.

statements.pro: Too many residents are currently struggling to make ends meet, even though they are above the official income threshold.
statements.con: Poverty assistance is intended only for those who need help the most.

statements.title: The municipality of Deurne must distribute free tampons and sanitary pads to people who cannot afford them.
statements.pro: These are basic products that everyone must be able to obtain; lack of money should not be a problem.
statements.con: The municipality does not need to organize this separately. Other organizations can do it, for example the food bank.

statements.title: Not only at the Binderendreef but also at the Liesselseweg there should be a road tunnel for cars under the railway.
statements.pro: A tunnel increases traffic safety at this location. A tunnel provides better traffic flow. The double intersection with traffic lights and the level crossing currently cause congestion.
